main_sections.title: Papa's Spectacles
main_sections.poem_text:
Today our papa
Is searching everywhere,
He has lost his spectacles,
It was his only pair!

He can't read the newspaper,
He can't watch TV,
He can't cut the vegetables,
Oh! Where can it be?

He checked inside his pockets,
He glanced under his chair,
He searched near the window,
He looked everywhere.

I told him, "Stop, Papa!"
I went up close, and said!
"Papa, your spectacles
Are right there on your head!"
main_sections.poem_appreciation_summary: The poem is a humorous, lighthearted narrative about a father who frantically searches for his spectacles without realising they are perched on his head. Told from a child's point of view, it captures the warm domestic scene with gentle comedy. The central theme is the irony of searching for something that is in plain sight, paired with a tender moment of a child helping a helpless parent. The tone is playful and affectionate, with a bouncy four-beat rhythm that suits oral recitation. The dominant device is anaphora — the repeated 'He can't…' and 'He checked… He glanced… He searched…' build comic anticipation before the cheerful revelation in the final stanza. Well-suited for dramatic recitation with expressive gestures.

reading.section_name: Let us Recite + Let us Think A

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us Recite — Read the poem 'Papa's Spectacles' aloud with your class.

visual_stimulus.brief_description: Illustrations depicting a man searching for his spectacles in various places — near a window, under a chair, in his pockets — while a child looks on and finally points to the spectacles on his head.

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
A. Answer the following questions.
1. What is Papa searching for?
2. What are the possible places where Papa can find it?
3. At last, where does Papa find his lost item?

question_bank.stem: What is Papa searching for?
What are the possible places where Papa can find it?
At last, where does Papa find his lost item?

oracy.section_name: Let us Think B + Let us Speak + Let us Listen

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
B. Think and discuss.
1. Apart from reading, guess some other chores that Papa cannot do without the spectacles.
2. What makes your papa happy?
3. Make a list of things as instructed below.
[Table 1: Things generally misplaced at your home | Who helps in finding these misplaced things?]
How would you express your gratitude to them?
[Table 2: Things generally misplaced at your school | Who helps in finding these misplaced things?]
How would you express your gratitude to them?

question_bank.stem: Apart from reading, guess some other chores that Papa cannot do without the spectacles.
What makes your papa happy?
Make a list of things generally misplaced at your home and who helps in finding them. Then do the same for school. How would you express your gratitude to them?
question_bank.table:
Things generally misplaced at your home|Who helps in finding these misplaced things?
...|...
...|...
...|...
...|...

tasks_verbatim.task_text: 'Papa' is a commonly used word in English to address one's 'father'. Say aloud the word you use for 'father' in your language. Make a list of such words in your notebook. Share them with the class.

Your teacher will read aloud a few word pairs. Listen carefully and encircle pairs in which the words sound different.
pair-peer / fair-fare / sun-son / knot-note / ate-eight / hair-hare / deer-dear / dare-dear / bed-bad / fair-fear / cold-could / wood-would / knot-not / where-were / heal-heel / fill-feel
tasks_verbatim.transcript_text: Teacher reads each pair aloud 3–4 times: pair-peer, fair-fare, sun-son, knot-note, ate-eight, hair-hare, deer-dear, dare-dear, bed-bad, fair-fear, cold-could, wood-would, knot-not, where-were, heal-heel, fill-feel. Students listen and encircle pairs where the two words sound different (i.e. are not homophones). Note to teacher: repeat each pair 3–4 times; use audio if available.

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
A. Look at the words in each group. Arrange them in ascending order (Write the smallest or lightest object first and the biggest or heaviest last.)
1. Towel, Sari, Handkerchief, Bedsheet
2. Apple, Strawberry, Cherry, Watermelon
3. Twenty-nine, Fourteen, Ninety-one, Forty-seven
4. Hen, Crow, Sparrow, Eagle
5. Ox, Rabbit, Elephant, Squirrel
Write two examples more, following the same pattern.

question_bank.stem: Arrange in ascending order (smallest/lightest first): Towel, Sari, Handkerchief, Bedsheet
Arrange in ascending order (smallest/lightest first): Apple, Strawberry, Cherry, Watermelon
Arrange in ascending order (smallest/lightest first): Twenty-nine, Fourteen, Ninety-one, Forty-seven
Arrange in ascending order (smallest/lightest first): Hen, Crow, Sparrow, Eagle
Arrange in ascending order (smallest/lightest first): Ox, Rabbit, Elephant, Squirrel
Write your own example following the same pattern (example 1).
Write your own example following the same pattern (example 2).

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
B. Note the following phrase: a pair of shoes. The word 'pair' is used in all the phrases given below. Fill in the blanks and complete the phrases. [Pictures shown: trousers, scissors, spectacles, socks, gloves, earrings, badminton rackets]
1. a pair of ________
2. a pair of ________
3. a pair of ________
4. a pair of ________
5. a pair of ________
6. a pair of ________
7. a pair of ________

question_bank.stem: a pair of ________ (picture: trousers)
a pair of ________ (picture: scissors)
a pair of ________ (picture: spectacles/glasses)
a pair of ________ (picture: socks)
a pair of ________ (picture: gloves)
a pair of ________ (picture: earrings)
a pair of ________ (picture: badminton rackets)

visual_stimulus.brief_description: Individual pictures of paired objects: trousers, scissors, spectacles, socks, gloves, earrings, badminton rackets — one per blank.

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
C. Encircle the word that is spelt correctly. One has been done for you.
1. watter / water / weter
2. vegatables / veggetables / vegetables
3. balloon / baloon / ballon
4. hause / house / houze
5. elephant / elefant / ellephant
6. spactacles / spektacles / spectacles
7. karpenter / carpenter / carpainter
8. fruitt / friut / fruit
9. receive / recieve / raceive
10. prinsipal / principal / princpal

question_bank.stem: Encircle the correctly spelt word: watter / water / weter
Encircle the correctly spelt word: vegatables / veggetables / vegetables
Encircle the correctly spelt word: balloon / baloon / ballon
Encircle the correctly spelt word: hause / house / houze
Encircle the correctly spelt word: elephant / elefant / ellephant
Encircle the correctly spelt word: spactacles / spektacles / spectacles
Encircle the correctly spelt word: karpenter / carpenter / carpainter
Encircle the correctly spelt word: fruitt / friut / fruit
Encircle the correctly spelt word: receive / recieve / raceive
Encircle the correctly spelt word: prinsipal / principal / princpal

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
D. Complete the story using the correct prepositions. [Word bank: around, after, before, in, above, on, among]
My father has a habit of keeping his spectacles ________ random places. Last week, he could not find his spectacles and looked ________ the house. We checked the big box that reached ________ our waists. My father looked for the spectacles ________ the newspapers kept ________ the table. He continued searching for two hours ________ finally giving up. He sat down on the bed ________ giving one final look to the room and said with a sigh, 'Looks like it is time to get a new pair.'

question_bank.stem: My father has a habit of keeping his spectacles ________ random places.
…he could not find his spectacles and looked ________ the house.
We checked the big box that reached ________ our waists.
My father looked for the spectacles ________ the newspapers kept ________ the table.
He continued searching for two hours ________ finally giving up.
He sat down on the bed ________ giving one final look to the room.

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
E. Use the clues given below to complete the crossword puzzle!
Across: 2. Not far, it's ________. 6. I want ________ clothes, not the dirty ones. 8. Not inside, play ________. 9. When it's not bright, it's ________.
Down: 1. I said no but he said ________. 3. Something small is not ________. 4. Don't be sad, be ________. 5. You are slow, she is ________. 6. Not hot, she likes ________ coffee. 7. Not bought, but ________.

question_bank.stem: Across 2: Not far, it's ________.
Across 6: I want ________ clothes, not the dirty ones.
Across 8: Not inside, play ________.
Across 9: When it's not bright, it's ________.
Down 1: I said no but he said ________.
Down 3: Something small is not ________.
Down 4: Don't be sad, be ________.
Down 5: You are slow, she is ________.
Down 6: Not hot, she likes ________ coffee.
Down 7: Not bought, but ________.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Write the poem Papa's Spectacles in the form of a story in your notebook. Share your story with the class. You may start like this: Today, my father is unhappy…

beyond_text.section_name: Let us Do + Just for Fun

tasks_verbatim.task_text: A. Fun with matchsticks. Move 3 matchsticks to make 3 squares.

visual_stimulus.brief_description: A 2×2 arrangement of 4 squares made from matchsticks — students must move 3 matchsticks to form exactly 3 squares.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: B. Look at the flame. Touch the red star with the tip of your nose, and look at the candle. And lo! It starts burning!

visual_stimulus.brief_description: An optical illusion: a drawing of a candle and a separate red star — when the viewer focuses on the red star close up and then shifts gaze to the candle, it appears to be lit.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: C. Look carefully. Can you find 'd'? [A 9×12 grid of 'b' letters with one 'd' hidden inside]

visual_stimulus.brief_description: A 9-row by 12-column grid filled with the letter 'b', with one 'd' hidden in row 4, column 2.

tasks_verbatim.task_text:
Just for Fun — Riddle:
I go to the market,
I lovingly bring you home,
I sit in front of you,
And I cry, cry, cry.
What is it?

question_bank.stem: I go to the market, I lovingly bring you home, I sit in front of you, And I cry, cry, cry. What is it?
